title: Bridge Essay: Neuroecology beyond the brain: learning in Echinodermata.
concept: 4E cognition — embodied, enactive, extended, embedded mind.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, enactive, extended, embedded mind.

**Neuroecology beyond the brain: learning in Echinodermata.** - Learn Behav (2022). DOI: 10.3758/s13420-021-00492-3. PMID: 34877627. We propose an expansion of neuroecological comparisons to include the capabilities of brainless and non-neural organisms. We begin this enterprise by conducting a systematic search for studies on learning in echinoderms. Echinodermata are marine invertebrates comprising starfish, brittle stars, sea cucumbers, sea urchins, and sea lilies.
